programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team looks into a raid on a naval base, and Dr Cyril Taft - the surgeon who saved Gibbs' life - experiences the thrill of assisting on an investigation. programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: First of a two-part story. The body of missing schoolgirl Chloe Parks is found at a derelict site, but Crime Scene Examiner Lorna Hart cannot confirm the cause of death. Suspicion immediately falls on the victim's boyfriend, who seems to be responsible for recent changes in her behaviour, but a chilling home video given to the police by a classmate changes the direction of the investigation. Chris Walker guest stars. Concludes tomorrow

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: It's Ben's christening, and Arthur collapses on his allotment. Cindy and David platonically celebrate their 'anniversary'

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Kathy and Phil's christening party for baby Ben goes with a swing but fate strikes a cruel blow at the height of the festivities

programme.title: Soldier, Soldier
programme.desc: Major Radley decides to stage an escape and evasion exercise. Meanwhile, Bernie Roberts is faced with a crisis. Starring Robson Green

programme.title: Pie in the Sky
programme.desc: Crabbe reluctantly attends a boring police management seminar, while business is booming back at the restaurant - although Margaret learns that success has its price. Drama, with Richard Griffiths, Malcolm Sinclair, Maggie Steed and Bella Enahoro

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Tristan is welcomed back to Darrowby, where neglected animals and tight-fisted farmers cause problems for the vets. Rural drama, starring Christopher Timothy, Robert Hardy and Peter Davison

programme.title: To the Manor Born
programme.desc: Richard's business empire begins to crumble, leaving him with little option but to sell Grantleigh Manor. On receiving an inheritance from her late uncle, Audrey is finally able to reclaim the estate - and DeVere. The original final episode of the 1970s sitcom, starring Penelope Keith and Peter Bowles

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: Mr Grace decides to cater for working people by opening the shop at 8.30 in the morning, much to the staff's displeasure. They wonder if this is the shape of things to come, or whether they can put paid to the plans to boost profits. Comedy, with John Inman, Mollie Sugden and Trevor Bannister

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Clegg, Compo and Blamire look for a piano to provide the entertainment at Gloria's surprise farewell party before she emigrates to Australia. Meanwhile, two new librarians arrive and are bent on keeping the morality of the facility in tact

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Thinking they have to much free time on their hands, the trio decide to find some kind of occupation. They encounter a dodgy bankrupted dealer who persuades them to sell his new product - with disastrous results, particularly for Blamire. Michael Bates, Bill Owen and Peter Sallis star

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: At the behest of a local businessman, unlikely manhunter Hetty fearlessly sets out in search of the thugs responsible for attacking Asian businessmen. She soon discovers that every victim has been a restaurant owner, but then Hetty herself becomes a target of the gang involved

programme.title: Life on Mars
programme.desc: Sam is called to a murder at a textiles factory, which he is shocked to discover will be his own home in the 21st century. He puts his knowledge of forensics into use to find the killer, but Gene has a simpler approach - the first suspect to speak did it. As battle lines are drawn across CID, it becomes increasingly clear that the Seventies method of detection may in this case be right. John Simm stars

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: After being released from jail, notorious paedophile John Davies admits to the abduction and murder of a child 25 years previously. Suspicious of his motives, the detectives reopen the unsolved case in an unusual attempt to prove his innocence, reinvestigating the boy's disappearance while on a demonstration with his left-wing activist parents. Crime drama, guest starring Adrian Schiller and Samantha Bond

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Boyd expects a confession of guilt when he is summoned to prison by one of the inmates, a former policeman serving life for the murder of his partner. The convict does make a revelation - but what he has to say causes the detective out to re-examine the facts of the old case from every possible angle to assure himself of the man's guilt. Trevor Eve and Felicite du Jeu star

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: First of a two-part story. The body of missing schoolgirl Chloe Parks is found at a derelict site, but Crime Scene Examiner Lorna Hart cannot confirm the cause of death. Suspicion immediately falls on the victim's boyfriend, who seems to be responsible for recent changes in her behaviour, but a chilling home video given to the police by a classmate changes the direction of the investigation. programme.desc: Iolo Williams travels to Ireland, where he explores the country's wildlife and landscape. He visits the Skellig Islands and Killarney in Co Kerry, the Burren near Doolin in Co Clare, Ballycroy in Co Mayo and Boffin Island, off the west coast of Connemara

programme.title: Blociau Rhif

programme.title: Caru Canu a Stori

programme.title: Cacamwnci

programme.title: Dennis a Dannedd

programme.title: Seligo

programme.title: Mastermind Plant Cymru

programme.title: Ffeil
programme.desc: Daily news and sport headlines aimed at a young audience

programme.desc: Geraint Hardy journeys around Wales, and in this first edition he sees what Abergavenny has to offer

programme.title: Be' Ti'n Gwylio?
programme.desc: A mother and son from Ammanford, four housemates in Cardiff and a family from Nelson compete from the comfort of their own homes to win a takeaway of their choice

programme.title: Newyddion S4C

programme.title: Heno
programme.desc: Magazine programme, featuring stories from all over Wales

programme.title: Newyddion S4C a'r Tywydd
programme.desc: News and weather

programme.title: Sain yn 50
programme.desc: The year 2019 saw Sain - Wales' most iconic record label - celebrate its 50th anniversary. This documentary captures a landmark event for a company that's provided a launchpad for some of the county's most popular artists and championed Welsh language music across the world. Contributors include Bryn Terfel, Caryl Parry Jones, Elin Fflur and the label's founders Huw Jones and Dafydd Iwan

programme.title: Newyddion S4C a'r Tywydd
programme.desc: News and weather

programme.title: Hewlfa Drysor
programme.desc: Game show in which three teams drive round different areas of Wales in search of treasure, combining their general knowledge skills, understanding of history and map-reading skills as they race to get there first. Here, Lisa Angharad and the Welsh Whisperer take the challenge to Llangernyw to see who will become car treasure hunt champions

programme.title: Noson Lawen
programme.desc: A host of talented singers and comedians come together to celebrate Dafydd Iwan's 75th birthday on the Noson Lawen. With Gwyneth Glyn, Twm Morys, Ifan Gruffydd, Delwyn Siôn, The Welsh Whisperer, Seingar Choir and the Bro Teifi School Choir. Presented by Elin Fflur

programme.title: Caru Siopa
programme.desc: Fashion presenter Lara Catrin heads to Llanelli to meet two women who love buying and styling clothes. Ffion and Hannah are challenged to use a budget of £300 to buy items from charity and second-hand shops before transforming them and selling them on in a vintage fair in town. Ffion's a fashion design student, while Hannah's a part-time model. Who will come out on top?

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Gethin Jones presents the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters follow the development progress of properties sold at auction in Blairgowrie in Perth and Kinross, Fenton in Stoke-on-Trent and Chatham in Kent

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Catching up with Steve and Diane Hargreaves three years after they appeared on the show to see if they decided to leave the UK behind for a new life in Australia

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: A ruthless gang are caught attacking people who are wearing expensive watches. Plus, a look at some of the terrible disguises criminals have used to try and hide their identities

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Caroline Hawley and David Harper give advice to two teams at Newbury Showground. Plus, host Eric Knowles learns of some fascinating hoards found in the local area

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: When a tortured artist is murdered, Father Brown must discover who committed the crime before it is too late. Starring Mark Williams and Sorcha Cusack

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Bakeation
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers continue their journey in Germany, proving that German cuisine isn't all sausages and beer, but is based on regional specialities

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps buyers who want to relocate from Surrey to north Devon on a £650,000 budget, and visits the annual regatta in the fishing village of Appledore

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Beekeepers Debbie and Chris collect honey from their hives, while vegetable and fruit farmers Paul and Jackie gather their crops for the Winchester Farmer's Market

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: The dealers bid against each other on items including a unique 1960s vase, a curious mirror, two chalkware statues and a troll called Trotter

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Party Political Broadcast
programme.desc: By the Liberal Democrats

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio. Presented by Alex Jones

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers presents as five dealers bid against one another to buy intriguing items. First to arrive is Princess from Surrey, with an unusual item - a set of wooden, carved apples, which are cut into different numbers of sections and were used to help children learn about fractions. Next, part-time entertainer Chris brings in a bed of nails, before 18-year-old Jude presents the dealers with a beautifully preserved, original account of a First World War Zeppelin raid on Southend

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team of experts bring four treasured family heirlooms back to life. First up, Rose Werner and her sister Linda hope that Suzie Fletcher can turn her expertise to a decorative leather box that they hold dear to their hearts. Peter Coxon is next to arrive, hoping the teamwork of Steve Fletcher and Dominic Chinea can provide a speedy recovery to a favourite childhood toy, a tin speed boat. Later, the experts also turn their attention to an Italian dressing table chair and a tortoise pouffe

programme.title: Celebrity Best Home Cook
programme.desc: The remaining nine talented celebrity home cooks are put through their paces as they face a series of challenges. First, Mary Berry tasks the famous faces to make a chocolate dessert fit for a romantic date night, before the competitors take on Chris Bavin's tricky challenge of rustling up a dish using his favourite cut of leftover meat. The judges must then decide, taking both challenges into account, who is safe and who needs to prove themselves by cooking again in Angela Hartnett's dreaded Eliminator

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures, including Manchester United v Sheffield United at Old Trafford. Both teams were in need of points for different reasons, with the Red Devils challenging for the title and the Blades struggling at the foot of the table. Plus, Everton v Leicester City, Burnley v Aston Villa, Chelsea v Wolverhampton Wanderers and Crystal Palace v West Ham United

programme.title: Fight for First: Excel Esports
programme.desc: Excel prepare for one of their biggest challenges of the season - a face-off with their UK rivals and former World Champions Fnatic. Not only is this an all-important match, but coach 'Young Buck' has his reputation to play for. He brought Fnatic to glory as their coach in the World Championships, but can Joey beat his old team with the current underdogs of the LEC?

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Former England striker Darren Bent, Great Britain 110m hurdles great Colin Jackson, three-time world gymnastics champion Beth Tweddle and former swimming star Karen Pickering join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Gethin Jones hosts the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The lowdown on a rundown home in Cranbrook, Kent, as well as renovations in Chaddesden in Derby and Aberdare in Wales. Presented by Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martell Maxwell

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman catches up with former Olympic diver Ben Swain, who hoped he and his wife could make a fresh start in Australia after an injury cut short his sporting career

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: A woman is shocked to find out her new best friend is a fraud, an airport bag thief steals something irreplaceable and clumsy crooks crash through the ceiling of a restaurant

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: The red and blue teams vie at Brighton Racecourse, East Sussex, hunting for items of value with the help of experts Catherine Southon and Mark Stacey. Presenter Christina Trevanion maintains order and also shows off a tasty mystery item

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: A gossip columnist is murdered after threatening to expose a fashion house's secrets, leaving the sleuthing priest with a fresh mystery to solve. Whodunit, starring Mark Williams

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Bakeation
programme.desc: Slovakia, Hungary and Romania mark the halfway point in Si King and Dave Myers' baking odyssey. They begin in a cafe in Bratislava, where they are served three cakes inspired by British TV cookery shows, then taste kremesh, the Hungarian version of vanilla slice. They also try a roadside snack of deep-fried pastry with sour cream and cheese, an Eastern European take on bread-and-butter pudding, and cheese and bacon savoury scones, before ending this leg of their journey with a family party thrown by Dave's Romanian wife Lil

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley looks for a property in Perth & Kinross for a couple who have a £450,000 budget. Along the way, she gets up close and personal with some of the region's tallest trees

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: The documentary follows three women attending Stroud Farmers' Market to sell their produce. Sisters Rowan and Lily took over their mother's business creating essential oils from plants grown in their garden. Now they are learning the ropes while trying to keep the business afloat. Meanwhile, dairy farmer Jess is selling produce from her cows - who she considers to be her 'business partners'. Narrated by Denis Lawson

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: The dealers bid against each other on a pair of 1930s suitcases, a wind-up tin toy, a magic lantern, a silver condiment holder and a table and chair made from a fairground car

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Party Political Broadcast
programme.desc: By the Green Party

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones hosts the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Love is in the air as Ben, with a little help from Lexi and Lola, puts his engagement plan into action. However, Callum has doubts over his recent double-crossing of the Mitchells. Whitney tells Gray she has a date, so he plants a seed of doubt in her mind. Honey and Jay decide the time has come to tell Billy the truth about their relationship, while Chelsea convinces Lucas to make amends with Denise, Jack and Patrick

programme.title: Pooch Perfect
programme.desc: Sheridan Smith and her canine co-host Stanley open the doors of the Pooch Parlour to the last of the 16 dog groomers. First they tackle a quartet of cute Pomeranians, having to achieve a perfect pom-pom ball shape using scissors only. Then four party pooches hit the Dogwalk as a family of poodles and a Yorkshire terrier strut their stuff, having been given a makeover by the contestants. Who will judges Verity Hardcastle and Colin Taylor choose to go through to the quarter-finals?

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Neville tries to embrace island life with a game of beach volleyball, only for a rogue sandfly bite to land him in hospital. His time on the ward takes a shocking turn when a nurse takes her own life - although the DI suspects foul play and starts to build a case against his key suspects. Meanwhile, JP and Marlon are struggling to achieve a good working relationship

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: Katie Price: Harvey and Me
programme.desc: Model and businesswoman Katie Price's son Harvey was born with a rare disorder that affects brain function, hormones and vision, and he has a long list of medical and behavioural conditions that mean his future is unpredictable. This documentary follows Katie and Harvey as he turns 18, with the pair exploring the options of further education colleges and meeting other families that contain disabled young adults

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Gethin Jones hosts the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Dion Dublin take a look around a shell of a property in Bilston near Wolverhampton, a bungalow in Lower Quinton, near Stratford-upon-Avon, and an end terrace in in Gorton, Manchester

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Gill Johnson was convinced Australia would be the perfect place for her and her family to settle down. Husband Mark, however, was less than enthusiastic. Would a week in Perth persuade him that she wasn't just on a flight of fancy? Three years since their trial week, Nicki Chapman catches up with the pair to see whether they made the move Gill so desperately wanted

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood reports on how a detective's extraordinary ability to read body language brought a pair of international crooks to justice. Plus, the burglar who tried to batter his way into a chip shop and how inebriated night clubbers got a big shock when they took a short cut home

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Caroline Hawley and Phillip Serrell are on hand as two teams test their antiques knowledge at the Three Counties Showground in Malvern, Worcestershire. The competing duos then head to auction in Derbyshire. Presenter Charlie Ross oversees proceedings and also finds out about a classic car collection

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: After the body of Reginald Brody is discovered at the bottom of Helmsley Tower, Chief Inspector Valentine returns to Kembleford to investigate. Suspecting that there is more to his death than suicide, Valentine enlists Father Brown to help his inquiries. Mark Williams and Hugo Speer star in the crime drama

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Bakeation
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers reach Austria - the home of Mozart, the Sound of Music - on the fifth leg of their biking excursion across Europe. The trip starts in Vienna, where the pair unravel the mystery of the two competing Sachertorte recipes and taste the latest version - Sachertorte Reloaded

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley heads to Somerset to help a couple with a £310,000 budget find a country home where character charm and a practical utility room are top of the wish list. Along the way, Ginny heads into the Somerset Levels to see how bird wildlife efforts have brought the endangered crane back from near extinction

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Two Scottish farming families hope to make healthy sales at the Bowhouse market, Fife. To try to squeeze more income from their milk, husband and wife Robert and Jane, along with their son Ben now make 27 tonnes of award-winning cheese each year on their 660-acre dairy farm. Meanwhile, the Bruces gave up careers to start a small farm growing botanicals to make into spirits and liquors. Narrated by Denis Lawson

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers welcomes five more sellers to the Bidding Room, where they try to sell items to five professional dealers who are on the lookout for interesting pieces, with help from expert Simon Bower. Here, the dealers bid against each other on a vintage pub game, a small and mysterious statue and an apothecary set complete with tiny weights

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones presents the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich head to Hertfordshire to come up with design ideas for a large garden in Hemel Hempstead. Its owners, Carly and Andy, are keen to tackle an eyesore dating back to the Second World War

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic 4x100m gold medallist Mark Lewis-Francis, swimming great Rebecca Adlington and Scottish internationals in football and rugby union respectively Charlie Adam and John Barclay join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: A broken Billy snaps at Jay, and tells Lola about Jay and Honey - leaving his granddaughter in his bad books when he realises she already knew. Gray is happy to see how well Whitney is fitting in to her role and offers her a proposition, while Chelsea and Lucas wait for Jack

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedians Miles Jupp and Bob Mortimer and actresses Samantha Morton and Sarah Hadland join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds such as Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: When Lee joins Facebook for the first time and is contacted by an ex-girlfriend from 20 years ago, Lucy is annoyed that she cares so much. Her feelings are complicated by the sudden reappearance of a friend from University with whom she had a brief relationship. Threatened by fond memories from the past, Lee and Lucy start behaving badly. Comedy, starring Lee Mack and Sally Bretton

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by Hollywood star Sam Neill, who is appearing in new Australian comedy-drama Rams, and actress Billie Piper, who talks about her film Rare Beasts, which she wrote, directed and stars in. Cold Feet star James Nesbitt discusses his new Northern Ireland-set BBC series Bloodlands, while comedian Ando Osho promotes her debut novel Asking for a Friend. Plus, music legend Tom Jones chats and performs his new single No Hole in My Head

programme.title: Young Guns (1988)
programme.desc: Petty crook Billy the Kid is taken in by English farmer John Tunstall and given a job as a ranch hand. However, when Tunstall is murdered by a business rival, Billy and the rest of his 'regulators' set out to avenge his death, leading them into conflict with the law - and marking the start of Billy's notoriety as a gunslinger. Western, with Emilio Estevez, Charlie Sheen, Kiefer Sutherland and Lou Diamond Phillips

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman catches up with the Langridge family three years on from their trial week in Perth intended to decide if moving to Australia was right for them

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Donna and her sons go to sell their bacon butties, pulled pork wraps and burgers at Cullompton Food Festival. while Melanie and Peter are looking to sell their unique goat products

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: Young deaf activists Hamza, James, and Abigail learn what needs to be done to improve the future of their community. With voiceover

programme.title: Nigella's Cook, Eat, Repeat
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson shares recipes featuring one of her favourite ingredients - the anchovy. She prepares a creamy dressing for the fish, served with bitter leaves and walnuts, and then creates an elixir, combining silvery salted anchovies with garlic and oil. This not only makes an excellent dip for red chicory, but also the perfect accompaniment to a platter of sliced seared steak. Nigella creates an anchovy pasta dish of spaghetti with chard and chilli, and also makes a lemon blossom cocktail and a lemon and elderflower pudding

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: BBC Bitesize Daily
programme.desc: Maths, Science and T&D and Wellbeing Wednesday with Katie today is all about resilience

programme.title: Inside the Human Body
programme.desc: Michael Mosley traces human development from birth to adulthood, exploring how a newborn makes sense of the world and revealing how many brain connections are lost between the ages of 11 and 20 as an essential part of growing up. Plus, Moken sea gypsy children who train themselves to see clearly underwater and a Vietnamese girl who speaks 11 languages

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: In Sommieres, Paul Hayes caters for the Gallic masses with a 50-piece dinner service, and John Cameron spends big on a huge male torso sculpture

programme.title: Hidden Kingdoms
programme.desc: Documentary revealing the survival techniques used by the animal kingdom's smallest creatures. North America's Sonoran Desert is home to the grasshopper mouse, which is capable of disarming scorpions and centipedes twice its size, and displays a power and athleticism that enables it to avoid the attentions of rattlesnakes and tarantulas. In Africa, the elephant shrew, known as the sengi, employs speed to keep its distance from predators, following a meticulously constructed - and maintained - network of tiny racetracks in the savannah. The film concludes with Hidden Kingdoms Revealed, a 10-minute report on how the documentary was made. Narrated by Stephen Fry

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Winter returns and an abandoned Mozart tries to look after her newborn pups alone. Meanwhile, the rival Commandos force a confrontation with the Whiskers. Narrated by Bill Nighy

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Interesting finds from the show's travels on visits to Muncaster Castle in Cumbria, the Bowes Museum in Co Durham, the Grand Pier in Weston-super-Mare in Somerset and Norwich Cathedral. Items range from an art deco pair of Lalique vases to a 9.5mm projector. Presenter Paul Martin also visits Powis Castle in mid-Wales and finds out about their collection of Indian artefacts

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Charlie Brooks, Les Dennis, Darren Harriott and Melinda Messenger go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts a quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.desc: The Arden family experience corner shop life in the 1980s and 1990s, when stores changed from primary shopping destinations to a place for last minute impulse buys and were forced to make the maximum use of their floor space to stock thousands of products. The programme examines the impact of new legislation allowing supermarkets to open on Sundays, previously the only advantage small businesses had over them. ABC's Martin Fry visits the shop and reveals how the band used to use empty industrial units as cheap rehearsal space, and there is a look at the rise of the gaming industry and a revival of a famous 1980s marketing stunt

programme.title: Winterwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham is live in the New Forest, looking at what's been happening in the badger sett and at the latest action from the River Ness. Elsewhere, Gillian Burke is at RSPB Old Moor, where she explores the life-cycle of the eel, and Megan McCubbin goes swimming with jellyfish in a bid to understand why they are misunderstood

programme.title: DeLorean: Back from the Future
programme.desc: Documentary charting the rise and fall of John Z DeLorean and his doomed attempt to build the sports car of the future in 1980s war-torn Belfast. The programme features rare and unseen footage filmed by Oscar winning directors DA Pennebaker and Chris Hegedus, offering the first in-depth psychological profile of DeLorean

programme.title: Tomorrow's Worlds: The Unearthly History of Science Fiction
programme.desc: Dominic Sandbrook concludes his exploration of the sci-fi genre by considering the allure of time travel, with the power to change the past or see the future being a theme that writers and film-makers have embraced for many years. From HG Wells' pioneering scientist in The Time Machine, to Doc and Marty McFly in Back to the Future, audiences have been presented with a host of colourful travellers and their machines. Featuring contributions by former Doctor Who stars David Tennant and Karen Gillan as well as current showrunner Steven Moffat, actor Christopher Lloyd and screenwriter Bob Gale (Back to the Future), Quantum Leap star Scott Bakula and The Time Traveler's Wife author Audrey Niffenegger

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: The Last Survivors
programme.desc: Documentary compiling the personal testimonies of the remaining Holocaust survivors living in Britain, all of whom were children during their incarceration in the concentration camps. They discuss how these experiences continue to influence their adult lives and why particular memories have stayed with them. The film follows one man on a return journey to Auschwitz, accompanied by his daughter, while another survivor revisits his home town in Germany for the first time since 1946

programme.title: A Perfect Planet
programme.desc: Examining the solar force that powers Earth and how plants and animals have adapted to the uneven amounts of sunlight given to different parts of the planet over the course of a year. The exception is on the equator, where the duration of day and night remain the same throughout the year. Here, this guaranteed sunlight drives the great diversity of life in the tropical jungles

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: How a satnav revealed the mastermind behind the biggest armed robbery ever to hit Devon and Cornwall, and a hoodie helped police take a dangerous attacker off the streets

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: Young deaf activists Hamza, James, and Abigail learn what needs to be done to improve the future of their community. With voiceover

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Catching up with Steve and Diane Hargreaves three years after they appeared on the show to see if they decided to leave the UK behind for a new life in Australia

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Beekeepers Debbie and Chris collect honey from their hives, while vegetable and fruit farmers Paul and Jackie gather their crops for the Winchester Farmer's Market

programme.title: Pooch Perfect
programme.desc: Sheridan Smith and her canine co-host Stanley open the doors of the Pooch Parlour to four more professional dog groomers out to prove their skills. First they tackle a pack of Alaskan malamutes, whose fur well and truly flies when the contestants get to work de-shedding their undercoats. They then give four fluffy puppies their first ever haircut to be revealed in front of their proud owners. Verity Hardcastle and Colin Taylor judge their efforts and decide who is going through to the next round

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: BBC Bitesize Daily
programme.desc: Presented by Gethin Jones

programme.title: The Human Body: Secrets of Your Life Revealed
programme.desc: Chris and Xand van Tulleken explore the secrets of the body, beginning with the changes it goes through. They examine why human childhood is longer than any other creature on earth, reveal the communities of microbes - the microbiome - that people cultivate throughout their lives, and uncover the mysterious trigger from child to adult

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Paul Hayes and John Cameron visit an auction in Warrington and go head to head over a cameo brooch, and Paul resorts to sneaky tactics to hide items from his rival

programme.title: Hidden Kingdoms
programme.desc: Stephen Fry narrates another documentary revealing the survival techniques of the animal kingdom's smallest creatures. This film follows a young tree shrew as it darts through the rainforest of Borneo on the lookout for food. The creature's super-fast metabolism means it is always hungry, but as it searches for fruit, it must avoid fire ants, giant snakes and even a carnivorous plant. Cameras also follow a male chipmunk collecting acorns in preparation for winter - but while he's out, a bigger, more aggressive neighbour ransacks his store. Can he get the nuts back?

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Flower leads the Whiskers to better hunting grounds. However, the gang strays into the territory of the rival Zappa clan, and the ensuing battle leaves them searching for a missing pup

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin recalls trips to Muncaster Castle in Cumbria, the Bowes Museum in Co Durham, the Grand Pier in Weston-super-Mare in Somerset and Norwich Cathedral, where antiques experts inspected items ranging from an antique tipstaff to a piece of Bernard Leach pottery

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Charlie Brooks, Les Dennis, Darren Harriott and Melinda Messenger test their skills in a variety of trivia-based games, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts the quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions

programme.title: Inside Culture with Mary Beard
programme.desc: The classicist considers the rapidly changing language. Ian Hislop joins in a debate about the development of English due to texting and Twitter, which has led to a stand-off to grammar sticklers and language radicals. Mary also talks to musician Evelyn Glennie, who is profoundly deaf and communicates using touch and vibrations. Mary asks how life has changed for her at a time when everyone is communicating through cold, hard screens. Evelyn also presents an exclusive performance from Ely Cathedral

programme.title: Nigella: At My Table
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson serves up five more easy-to-make dishes, recipes that combine the comfort of the familiar and the exuberance of the new, rustling up her favourite weekend breakfast special in the shape of waffles. She also prepares dishes perfect for laidback entertaining in beef and aubergine fatteh, accompanied by bulgur wheat with flaked almonds and nigella seeds, followed by her passion fruit ice-cream cake. Late-night inspiration comes in the form of a toasted brie, parma ham and fig sandwich

programme.title: Winterwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham and the team present an edition focusing on wellbeing for both humans and animals, and how sometimes nature can have the answers. Megan McCubbin looks at the deep intelligence of pigeons, and why they should be admired, and there's a report from a woman who goes wild swimming with dolphins, something that has proved of enormous benefit to her mental health. Iolo Williams is at the Centre for Alternative technology in Wales and Chris rounds up the latest footage from the cameras in the New Forest

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig asks questions about rocks, rolls and rock 'n' roll, leaving Bill Bailey, Katy Brand, Eshaan Akbar and Alan Davies trying to come up with interesting answers - while avoiding the most obvious ones

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain hosts as Angela Barnes, Ed Gamble, Rhys James, Milton Jones and Maisie Adam join regular panellist Hugh Dennis to take a comic look back on the latest events in news and politics. Once again, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on one another

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: A look back at the year that arguably marked the peak of new British pop as Wham!, ABC and Culture Club all made appearances. Dexys Midnight Runners' new Irish sound topped the summer charts for four weeks with Come on Eileen and their follow-up, Jackie Wilson Says, led to a notorious moment in TOTP history. It was also the year that Shalamar's Jeffrey Daniel introduced the moonwalk to British TV

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emma Barnett

programme.title: The Trump Show
programme.desc: The last months of Donald Trump's presidency, following his efforts to overthrow the results of the 2020 election and the subsequent violent insurrection against the US Congress. Featuring contributions from advisors and close observers, and a look behind the scenes at the crowded rallies Trump held while the nation was suffering through the Covid-19 pandemic

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: A teenage paddleboarder is being swept out to sea off the coast of north Wales, so the crew of the Abersoch lifeboat race to reach him before he is lost for ever. The crew of the Lizard lifeboat station face two missions, as they are called out to a cargo ship taking on water and in danger of sinking with its crew still on board, followed by a small yacht being battered by a storm in the Atlantic

programme.title: Celebrity: A 21st Century Story
programme.desc: Having learned how to sidestep the conventional media machinery, celebrities acquired the tools take charge of their own narrative on social media. In 2016, actress Meghan Markle became Prince Harry's girlfriend. They publicly confirmed their relationship and swiftly denounced the press. Meanwhile, Donald Trump became US President, taking everything he'd learned from business and media to his role, including catchphrases and shock tactics. Ed Balls showed his moves on the Strictly dancefloor, Rachel Johnson entered the Big Brother house, and her father Stanley the I'm a Celebrity jungle, so while celebrities started playing politics, politicians were seemingly behaving more and more like celebrities

programme.title: Before They Were Famous
programme.desc: Another collection of archive clips showing celebrities' embarrassing early screen appearances

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman catches up with former Olympic diver Ben Swain, who hoped he and his wife could make a fresh start in Australia after an injury cut short his sporting career

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: The documentary follows three women attending Stroud Farmers' Market to sell their produce. Sisters Rowan and Lily took over their mother's business creating essential oils from plants grown in their garden. Now they are learning the ropes while trying to keep the business afloat. Meanwhile, dairy farmer Jess is selling produce from her cows - who she considers to be her 'business partners'. Narrated by Denis Lawson

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four family heirlooms back to life. Dominic Chinea undertakes the challenging restoration of a Carrom board, a traditional Asian board game, while Steven Kember teams up with silversmith Brenton West to repair a precious silver menorah. Elsewhere, Steve Fletcher restores a small wooden duck once used in magic tricks, and bookbinder Christopher Shaw repairs a pair of photo albums charting Captain Scott's Antarctic expedition

programme.title: BBC Bitesize Daily
programme.desc: Presented by Gethin Jones

programme.title: The Human Body: Secrets of Your Life Revealed
programme.desc: Chris and Xand van Tulleken explore the processes keeping human beings alive, seeing how powerful reflexes of fear and disgust keep people safe from danger and revealing the mechanisms bodies call on to repair damage. Xand visits one of the most advanced laboratories in the world to test his body's ability to maintain its inner balance, while Chris learns how scientists in Stockholm are trying to help people with phobias by investigating the neural mechanisms involved in fear and ways to control it

programme.title: The Human Body: Secrets of Your Life Revealed
programme.desc: Chris and Xand van Tulleken explore the way experiences shape the mind and body on the journey from being the one of the most helpless organisms on Earth to the most sophisticated. The brothers examine how people develop new skills - whether that's riding a bike or learning to walk - and reveal the unexpected link between the heartbeat and the ability to talk, while also showing how memories are formed in the brain and how experiences continue to change the body and genes throughout a lifetime

programme.title: Best Bakes Ever
programme.desc: Actress and cake expert Jane Asher presents another selection of recipes from TV chefs, including the likes of James Martin, Mary Berry, Paul Hollywood, the Hairy Bikers, Raymond Blanc, Nigella Lawson, Tom Kerridge, Lorraine Pascale and Rachel Khoo

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Flower sets out to rescue the pups from the dangers of Zappa territory, but it is up to Mitch to complete the mission. Meanwhile, Mozart and Carlos make an appearance, now the leaders of their own groups. Narrated by Bill Nighy

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At Powderham Castle in Devon, antiques experts Will Axon and Mark Stacey find several treasures to take off to auction, including a shotgun case that belonged to a First World War hero and a pristine collection of Dinky toys. Plus, Paul Martin delves into the past of the castle, which has been home to the same family for more than 600 years, and uncovers a scandal that changed the course of that family's history

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Charlie Brooks, Les Dennis, Darren Harriott and Melinda Messenger go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games. Their scores are tallied across the week resulting in an overall champion

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts a quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.desc: The Arderns reflect on the lessons learned through the series, and how corner shops can continue to thrive in the changing world of 21st-century business. The programme examines how shops have reinvented themselves to compete with mini-supermarkets, the rise of zero-waste shops, and how other businesses, like cafes, coffee shops and micro pubs, are filling the role of community hub

programme.title: Winterwatch
programme.desc: Megan McCubbin joins Chris Packham in the New Forest, getting a final look at the animals they've been witnessing for the last couple of weeks. Meanwhile, Iolo Williams is at the Centre for Alternative Technology in Wales, hoping to see some amazing sights and Gillian Burke is at the Beaver Project in Cornwall

programme.title: The Investigation
programme.desc: With the volunteers and divers continuing their search for evidence, Jens explains to Jakob that it is very difficult without the exact trace of the route taken by the submarine. Soren Malling stars in this drama based on a true story. In Danish and Swedish

programme.title: The Investigation
programme.desc: Prompted by Joachim, Jens asks the Swedish police to borrow their cadaver dogs, not commonly used in Denmark. Jakob is successful in procuring a warrant from the court to search the suspect's hard drives and a chance meeting brings the involvement of Torben Vang, an oceanographer with expertise on the complex currents in the waters between Denmark and Sweden. In Danish and Swedish

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland
programme.desc: Crossing the Sea of the Hebrides, Paul Murton heads to the Isle of Ewe, and on to the Summer Isles

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Faisal Islam, the BBC's Economics Editor, reviews the day's important national and international news stories

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Part one of two. Sharon helps Admiral Adama lead a daring rescue mission without the support of Apollo, and Tyrol rushes to save Cally from execution. With Edward James Olmos, Mary McDonnell, Katee Sackhoff and Jamie Bamber

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Part two of two. The crew forms an alliance with insurgents of New Caprica, forcing the Cylons to take drastic steps as they begin to lose control in the battle. Lee struggles to protect the civilian fleet on its journey toward Earth. Starring Edward James Olmos and Jamie Bamber

programme.title: I Remember You (2017)
programme.desc: After a number of strange deaths in the area, a new psychiatrist discovers one of the deceased was obsessed with the disappearance of his 8-year-old son three years previously. Meanwhile, three city dwellers are restoring a house when inexplicable, supernatural events start to occur. It becomes clear that the answer to both of these mysteries lie with the disappearance of a boy 60 years earlier. Icelandic mystery horror, starring Johannes Haukur Johannesson and Elma Stefania Agustsdottir. In Icelandic

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: In-depth current affairs report covering a story behind the headlines

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: With millions of people swapping the gym for the living room during the Covid-19 pandemic, Mehreen Baig delves into the latest science to find the best way to get fit at home. She uncovers exciting new research testing the theory that just one minute of strength training per week on each main muscle group might reduce your risk of diabetes and heart disease, and also looks at the impact wearable fitness tech actually has on our willpower to stick to an exercise plan. Later, she investigates whether popular supplements like pre-trainers and protein shakes really work

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Diary of Anne Frank
programme.desc: Anne is amazed by the changes happening to her body and emotions. To her surprise, she realises her feelings toward Peter are altering and begins openly flirting with him, much to Edith and Otto's concern

programme.title: The Joy of Painting: Winter Specials
programme.desc: The artist demonstrates how to paint a cabin in a winter landscape, complete with soft misty background and rich trees and bushes

programme.title: Queen Victoria's Children
programme.desc: The concluding episode focuses on the monarch's sons, examining how they struggled to assert themselves in the shadow of their father. It explores Victoria's difficult relationships with the eldest, Bertie, whose sexual indiscretions she blamed for having fatally weakened her husband's health, and Leopold, who put up the most determined effort to break free from his mother's control

programme.title: The Windermere Children
programme.desc: In August 1945, psychologist Oscar Friedmann and his team of counsellors look after child survivors of the Holocaust who were sent to the Lake District to begin new lives. Friedmann and art therapist Marie Paneth, philanthropist Leonard Montefiore and sports coach Jock Lawrence have four months to help the children reclaim their lives, while waiting for news of their loved ones. Fact-based drama, starring Thomas Kretschmann, Romola Garai, Tim McInnerny and Iain Glen

programme.title: The Windermere Children: In Their Own Words
programme.desc: The story of the pioneering project to rehabilitate child survivors of the Holocaust on the shores of Lake Windermere, with first-hand testimonies from some of those who attended. Reflecting on the survivors' lives after Windermere, the film includes home-movie footage and footage of their annual reunions

programme.title: Life of a Mountain: A Year on Helvellyn
programme.desc: Documentary capturing a year in the life of the Lake District's most popular peak, featuring insights from those that live by, care for and visit the mountain

programme.title: Arena
programme.desc: To mark 75 years of the Radio 4 stalwart, another chance to see this 1982 Bafta-winning documentary that celebrated the 40th anniversary of the long-running Radio 4 show created and hosted by Roy Plomley. Featuring contributions by Paul McCartney, Frankie Howerd, Trevor Brooking, JK Galbraith, Russell Harty and Arthur Askey

programme.title: The Joy of Painting: Winter Specials
programme.desc: The artist demonstrates how to paint a cabin in a winter landscape, complete with soft misty background and rich trees and bushes

programme.title: Queen Victoria's Children
programme.desc: The concluding episode focuses on the monarch's sons, examining how they struggled to assert themselves in the shadow of their father. It explores Victoria's difficult relationships with the eldest, Bertie, whose sexual indiscretions she blamed for having fatally weakened her husband's health, and Leopold, who put up the most determined effort to break free from his mother's control

programme.title: The Diary of Anne Frank
programme.desc: Anne sets about editing her diary for publication after a broadcast is heard announcing the Dutch government wants accounts of the war from civilians. D-Day gives the families hope and they plan what they will do when the conflict is over, unaware the Nazis have been tipped off about their hiding place

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross creates a tranquil landscape, almost feathery in appearance, at the base of a towering mountain

programme.title: The Boy in the Striped Pyjamas (2008)
programme.desc: Bruno, a boy in Nazi Germany, moves to the country when his father is placed in charge of a concentration camp. While exploring the woods close to the facility's boundary, he spots a Jewish youngster being held there, and the two become friends - but Bruno remains innocently oblivious to the camp's true purpose. Drama, starring David Thewlis, Asa Butterfield and Jack Scanlon

programme.title: The Eichmann Show
programme.desc: Fact-based drama telling the story of American producer Milton Fruchtman and blacklisted documentary film-maker Leo Hurwitz, who overcame enormous obstacles to record the trial of Nazi war criminal Adolf Eichmann, one of the major organisers of the Holocaust. Footage of his trial in Jerusalem in 1961, was broadcast in 37 countries and was the first time survivors had spoken about the horrors of the concentration camps to a mass audience. Martin Freeman and Anthony LaPaglia star, with Rebecca Front

programme.title: Mark Kermode's Secrets of Cinema
programme.desc: The critic examines the qualities necessary for a film to become a cult classic, as well as the main types of a genre that is dictated by the audience rather than the film-maker. Marks reveals how some set out to shock and break taboos, while others are camp classics embraced by viewers who find new or hidden meanings in them

programme.title: Scandal & Beauty: Mark Gatiss on Aubrey Beardsley
programme.desc: The actor and writer explores the life of the Victorian artist who scandalised the art world of the 1890s, revealing a man who was a technological innovator and whose uncompromising attitude still feels remarkably. He follows Beardsley's footsteps from his childhood in Brighton, via notoriety among the decadence of London's fin de siècle, to his early death in France in 1898 at the age of just 25

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross creates a tranquil landscape, almost feathery in appearance, at the base of a towering mountain

programme.title: Handmade in Mexico
programme.desc: Following the creation of alebrijes, which are brightly coloured, fantastical creatures carved from copal wood and decorated in extremely detailed paintwork

programme.title: Life of a Mountain: A Year on Helvellyn
programme.desc: Documentary capturing a year in the life of the Lake District's most popular peak, featuring insights from those that live by, care for and visit the mountain

programme.title: Sound of Musicals with Neil Brand
programme.desc: The presenter explores how a new generation of composers transformed musical theatre by embracing more gritty, challenging subjects, from the mean streets of 1950s New York in West Side Story to the Dickensian London of British blockbuster Oliver! Neil learns the stories behind Broadway hits Fiddler on the Roof and A Chorus Line, and celebrates the ground-breaking work of composer and lyricist Stephen Sondheim. He also reveals the 'secrets' of some classic numbers with the help of performers Robert Lindsay and Frances Ruffelle

programme.title: Cilla at the BBC
programme.desc: A compilation of memorable TV appearances by Cilla Black from the BBC archives. The much-missed performer began her presenting career in 1968 on the BBC, having already become one of Britain's most popular singers. The programme features her appearances on Not Only, But Also, The Ken Dodd Show, Top of the Pops and The Royal Variety Performance as well as her self-titled series, plus her famous 1973 duet with Marc Bolan

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Mark Goodier hosts the edition first aired on July 19, with performances by artists including Paul Young, Inspiral Carpets, Dream Warriors, Craig McLachlan and Check 1-2, Partners in Kryme, Diana Ross, Paul Young, FAB featuring MC Parker, Madonna, Elton John and Paula Abdul

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Jackie Brambles hosts the edition first aired on July 26, with performances by artists including Blue Pearl, the Soup Dragons featuring Junior Reid, Paula Abdul, Technotronic featuring Ya Kid K, DNA featuring Suzanne Vega, MC Hammer, River City People and Partners in Kryme

programme.title: Hip Hop: The Songs That Shook America
programme.desc: A look at groundbreaking hip-hop songs, revisited by the artists behind them, that were pivotal to the evolution of American music and culture. The first episode features Kanye West's Grammy-winning 2004 release Jesus Walks, which stood out by placing West's personal interpretation of Christian faith at the centre of its message

programme.title: Hip Hop: The Songs That Shook America
programme.desc: A look at American rapper Kendrick Lamar's 2015 song Alright, which was inspired by a visit to South Africa. A song about hope amid personal struggles, it became a soundtrack adopted by Black Lives Matter

programme.title: Hip Hop at the BBC
programme.desc: Hip-hop performances through the decades from the BBC archives, including the Sugarhill Gang in 1979 and Run DMC, LL Cool J and Eric B & Rakim in the '80s. Plus, Ice T, Monie Love, Fugees and the Roots in the '90s and latterly Dr Dre featuring Eminem, Dizzee Rascal and Jay-Z

programme.title: Hits, Hype & Hustle: An Insider's Guide to the Music Business
programme.desc: Having spent over 25 years in the music industry, agent Emma Banks has worked with some of the world's most famous artists and seen first-hand the fine line between success and failure. The secret to success and stardom is an elusive formula of luck, timing talent as well as the team behind the talent - the record executives, label bosses and A&R gurus. Here, Emma offers a behind-the-scenes insight into how the industry finds talent and creates superstars. Contributors include Motown legend Martha Reeves, Blur's Alex James and Labelle's Nona Hendryx

programme.title: Cilla at the BBC
programme.desc: A compilation of memorable TV appearances by Cilla Black from the BBC archives. The much-missed performer began her presenting career in 1968 on the BBC, having already become one of Britain's most popular singers. The programme features her appearances on Not Only, But Also, The Ken Dodd Show, Top of the Pops and The Royal Variety Performance as well as her self-titled series, plus her famous 1973 duet with Marc Bolan

programme.title: Sound of Musicals with Neil Brand

Neil learns the stories behind Broadway hits Fiddler on the Roof and A Chorus Line, and celebrates the ground-breaking work of composer and lyricist Stephen Sondheim. programme.desc: A gossip columnist is murdered after threatening to expose a fashion house's secrets, leaving the sleuthing priest with a fresh mystery to solve. Whodunit, starring Mark Williams

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Bakeation
programme.desc: Slovakia, Hungary and Romania mark the halfway point in Si King and Dave Myers' baking odyssey. They begin in a cafe in Bratislava, where they are served three cakes inspired by British TV cookery shows, then taste kremesh, the Hungarian version of vanilla slice. They also try a roadside snack of deep-fried pastry with sour cream and cheese, an Eastern European take on bread-and-butter pudding, and cheese and bacon savoury scones, before ending this leg of their journey with a family party thrown by Dave's Romanian wife Lil

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley looks for a property in Perth & Kinross for a couple who have a £450,000 budget. Along the way, she gets up close and personal with some of the region's tallest trees

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: The documentary follows three women attending Stroud Farmers' Market to sell their produce. Sisters Rowan and Lily took over their mother's business creating essential oils from plants grown in their garden. Now they are learning the ropes while trying to keep the business afloat. Meanwhile, dairy farmer Jess is selling produce from her cows - who she considers to be her 'business partners'. Narrated by Denis Lawson

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: The dealers bid against each other on a pair of 1930s suitcases, a wind-up tin toy, a magic lantern, a silver condiment holder and a table and chair made from a fairground car

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Party Political Broadcast
programme.desc: By Plaid Cymru

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones hosts the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Love is in the air as Ben, with a little help from Lexi and Lola, puts his engagement plan into action. However, Callum has doubts over his recent double-crossing of the Mitchells. Whitney tells Gray she has a date, so he plants a seed of doubt in her mind. Honey and Jay decide the time has come to tell Billy the truth about their relationship, while Chelsea convinces Lucas to make amends with Denise, Jack and Patrick

programme.title: Pooch Perfect
programme.desc: Sheridan Smith and her canine co-host Stanley open the doors of the Pooch Parlour to the last of the 16 dog groomers. First they tackle a quartet of cute Pomeranians, having to achieve a perfect pom-pom ball shape using scissors only. Then four party pooches hit the Dogwalk as a family of poodles and a Yorkshire terrier strut their stuff, having been given a makeover by the contestants. Who will judges Verity Hardcastle and Colin Taylor choose to go through to the quarter-finals?

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Neville tries to embrace island life with a game of beach volleyball, only for a rogue sandfly bite to land him in hospital. His time on the ward takes a shocking turn when a nurse takes her own life - although the DI suspects foul play and starts to build a case against his key suspects. Meanwhile, JP and Marlon are struggling to achieve a good working relationship

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: Katie Price: Harvey and Me
programme.desc: Model and businesswoman Katie Price's son Harvey was born with a rare disorder that affects brain function, hormones and vision, and he has a long list of medical and behavioural conditions that mean his future is unpredictable. This documentary follows Katie and Harvey as he turns 18, with the pair exploring the options of further education colleges and meeting other families that contain disabled young adults

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Gethin Jones hosts the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Dion Dublin take a look around a shell of a property in Bilston near Wolverhampton, a bungalow in Lower Quinton, near Stratford-upon-Avon, and an end terrace in in Gorton, Manchester

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood reports on how a detective's extraordinary ability to read body language brought a pair of international crooks to justice. Plus, the burglar who tried to batter his way into a chip shop and how inebriated night clubbers got a big shock when they took a short cut home

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Caroline Hawley and Phillip Serrell are on hand as two teams test their antiques knowledge at the Three Counties Showground in Malvern, Worcestershire. programme.desc: The latest developments, including the daily Welsh Government news conference

programme.desc: After the body of Reginald Brody is discovered at the bottom of Helmsley Tower, Chief Inspector Valentine returns to Kembleford to investigate. programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Bakeation
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers reach Austria - the home of Mozart, the Sound of Music - on the fifth leg of their biking excursion across Europe. The trip starts in Vienna, where the pair unravel the mystery of the two competing Sachertorte recipes and taste the latest version - Sachertorte Reloaded

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Gill Johnson was convinced Australia would be the perfect place for her and her family to settle down. Husband Mark, however, was less than enthusiastic. Would a week in Perth persuade him that she wasn't just on a flight of fancy? Three years since their trial week, Nicki Chapman catches up with the pair to see whether they made the move Gill so desperately wanted

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Two Scottish farming families hope to make healthy sales at the Bowhouse market, Fife. To try to squeeze more income from their milk, husband and wife Robert and Jane, along with their son Ben now make 27 tonnes of award-winning cheese each year on their 660-acre dairy farm. Meanwhile, the Bruces gave up careers to start a small farm growing botanicals to make into spirits and liquors. Narrated by Denis Lawson

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers welcomes five more sellers to the Bidding Room, where they try to sell items to five professional dealers who are on the lookout for interesting pieces, with help from expert Simon Bower. Here, the dealers bid against each other on a vintage pub game, a small and mysterious statue and an apothecary set complete with tiny weights

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones presents the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Dirty Vegan
programme.desc: Dirty Sanchez daredevil-turned-vegan chef Mathew Pritchard cooks a power-packed meal for members of the Cardiff Devils Ice hockey team, with the aim of supercharging their sticks and skates as they take on their sporting rivals

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic 4x100m gold medallist Mark Lewis-Francis, swimming great Rebecca Adlington and Scottish internationals in football and rugby union respectively Charlie Adam and John Barclay join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: A broken Billy snaps at Jay, and tells Lola about Jay and Honey - leaving his granddaughter in his bad books when he realises she already knew. Gray is happy to see how well Whitney is fitting in to her role and offers her a proposition, while Chelsea and Lucas wait for Jack

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedians Miles Jupp and Bob Mortimer and actresses Samantha Morton and Sarah Hadland join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds such as Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: When Lee joins Facebook for the first time and is contacted by an ex-girlfriend from 20 years ago, Lucy is annoyed that she cares so much. Her feelings are complicated by the sudden reappearance of a friend from University with whom she had a brief relationship. Threatened by fond memories from the past, Lee and Lucy start behaving badly. Comedy, starring Lee Mack and Sally Bretton

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by Hollywood star Sam Neill, who is appearing in new Australian comedy-drama Rams, and actress Billie Piper, who talks about her film Rare Beasts, which she wrote, directed and stars in. Cold Feet star James Nesbitt discusses his new Northern Ireland-set BBC series Bloodlands, while comedian Ando Osho promotes her debut novel Asking for a Friend. Plus, music legend Tom Jones chats and performs his new single No Hole in My Head

programme.title: Rhod Gilbert: Stand Up to Infertility
programme.desc: With he and his wife struggling to conceive, the comedian investigates male infertility, setting out to discover why nobody seems to be addressing the issue despite it accounting for around 50 per cent of all fertility cases. He discovers that the fact men don't want to talk about infertility is doing just as much harm as the condition itself

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: Over the past 12 months, a team from Imperial College London, in collaboration with the BBC, have surveyed the mental health of over 350,000 people across the UK during the Covid-19 pandemic. Now, clinical psychologist Professor Tanya Byron joins former England footballer Alex Scott to discover how the latest science can help us gain greater control over our state of mind and improve mental health and wellbeing

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: When a tortured artist is murdered, Father Brown must discover who committed the crime before it is too late. Starring Mark Williams and Sorcha Cusack

programme.desc: A ruthless gang are caught attacking people who are wearing expensive watches. Plus, a look at some of the terrible disguises criminals have used to try and hide their identities

programme.title: Escape to the Country

programme.desc: Beekeepers Debbie and Chris collect honey from their hives, while vegetable and fruit farmers Paul and Jackie gather their crops for the Winchester Farmer's Market

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio. Presented by Alex Jones

programme.title: River City
programme.desc: Ruby reaches out to Caitlin after she is faced with the terrible truth about Joe, while Amber takes drastic action to gain respect from the Murdoch men in her life, and jealousy rears its head when Jac meets one of Lou's old flames

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team of experts bring four treasured family heirlooms back to life. First up, Rose Werner and her sister Linda hope that Suzie Fletcher can turn her expertise to a decorative leather box that they hold dear to their hearts. Peter Coxon is next to arrive, hoping the teamwork of Steve Fletcher and Dominic Chinea can provide a speedy recovery to a favourite childhood toy, a tin speed boat. Later, the experts also turn their attention to an Italian dressing table chair and a tortoise pouffe

programme.title: Celebrity Best Home Cook
programme.desc: The remaining nine talented celebrity home cooks are put through their paces as they face a series of challenges. First, Mary Berry tasks the famous faces to make a chocolate dessert fit for a romantic date night, before the competitors take on Chris Bavin's tricky challenge of rustling up a dish using his favourite cut of leftover meat. The judges must then decide, taking both challenges into account, who is safe and who needs to prove themselves by cooking again in Angela Hartnett's dreaded Eliminator

programme.title: Scot Squad
programme.desc: City cops Jack McLaren and Sarah Fletcher are called upon to deal with a delivery driver in distress. Hugh McKirdy saves a bride-to-be stranded at the roadside - only for his heartstrings to be pulled when her veil is lifted. Bobby and Officer Karen are locked in down at the station when new security measures cause confusion, Ken Beattie crushes a criminal in the cinema and Jane Mackay considers ending her partnership with Charlie McIntosh. Comedy, starring Jack Docherty, Grado and Karen Bartke

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures, including Manchester United v Sheffield United at Old Trafford. Both teams were in need of points for different reasons, with the Red Devils challenging for the title and the Blades struggling at the foot of the table. Plus, Everton v Leicester City, Burnley v Aston Villa, Chelsea v Wolverhampton Wanderers and Crystal Palace v West Ham United

programme.title: Scottish Questions
programme.desc: Ministers are put on the spot by fellow MPs in their monthly question-and-answer session at Westminster

programme.title: The Edit
programme.desc: The best of the week's entertainment news with Amy Irons

programme.title: Fight for First: Excel Esports
programme.desc: Excel prepare for one of their biggest challenges of the season - a face-off with their UK rivals and former World Champions Fnatic. Not only is this an all-important match, but coach 'Young Buck' has his reputation to play for. He brought Fnatic to glory as their coach in the World Championships, but can Joey beat his old team with the current underdogs of the LEC?

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Former England striker Darren Bent, Great Britain 110m hurdles great Colin Jackson, three-time world gymnastics champion Beth Tweddle and former swimming star Karen Pickering join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Young Guns (1988)
programme.desc: Petty crook Billy the Kid is taken in by English farmer John Tunstall and given a job as a ranch hand.
