programme.title: Celebrity Best Home Cook
programme.desc: The remaining nine talented celebrity home cooks are put through their paces as they face a series of challenges. First, Mary Berry tasks the famous faces to make a chocolate dessert fit for a romantic date night, before the competitors take on Chris Bavin's tricky challenge of rustling up a dish using his favourite cut of leftover meat. The judges must then decide, taking both challenges into account, who is safe and who needs to prove themselves by cooking again in Angela Hartnett's dreaded Eliminator

programme.title: The Top Table
programme.desc: Young people come to the Top Table to face down our politicians in an exciting audience debate show, presented by Stephen Nolan

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures, including Manchester United v Sheffield United at Old Trafford. Both teams were in need of points for different reasons, with the Red Devils challenging for the title and the Blades struggling at the foot of the table. Plus, Everton v Leicester City, Burnley v Aston Villa, Chelsea v Wolverhampton Wanderers and Crystal Palace v West Ham United

programme.title: Fight for First: Excel Esports
programme.desc: Excel prepare for one of their biggest challenges of the season - a face-off with their UK rivals and former World Champions Fnatic. Not only is this an all-important match, but coach 'Young Buck' has his reputation to play for. He brought Fnatic to glory as their coach in the World Championships, but can Joey beat his old team with the current underdogs of the LEC?

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Former England striker Darren Bent, Great Britain 110m hurdles great Colin Jackson, three-time world gymnastics champion Beth Tweddle and former swimming star Karen Pickering join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Gethin Jones hosts the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The lowdown on a rundown home in Cranbrook, Kent, as well as renovations in Chaddesden in Derby and Aberdare in Wales. Presented by Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martell Maxwell

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman catches up with former Olympic diver Ben Swain, who hoped he and his wife could make a fresh start in Australia after an injury cut short his sporting career

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: A woman is shocked to find out her new best friend is a fraud, an airport bag thief steals something irreplaceable and clumsy crooks crash through the ceiling of a restaurant

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: The red and blue teams vie at Brighton Racecourse, East Sussex, hunting for items of value with the help of experts Catherine Southon and Mark Stacey. Presenter Christina Trevanion maintains order and also shows off a tasty mystery item

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: A gossip columnist is murdered after threatening to expose a fashion house's secrets, leaving the sleuthing priest with a fresh mystery to solve. Whodunit, starring Mark Williams

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Bakeation
programme.desc: Slovakia, Hungary and Romania mark the halfway point in Si King and Dave Myers' baking odyssey. They begin in a cafe in Bratislava, where they are served three cakes inspired by British TV cookery shows, then taste kremesh, the Hungarian version of vanilla slice. They also try a roadside snack of deep-fried pastry with sour cream and cheese, an Eastern European take on bread-and-butter pudding, and cheese and bacon savoury scones, before ending this leg of their journey with a family party thrown by Dave's Romanian wife Lil

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley looks for a property in Perth & Kinross for a couple who have a £450,000 budget. Along the way, she gets up close and personal with some of the region's tallest trees

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: The documentary follows three women attending Stroud Farmers' Market to sell their produce. Sisters Rowan and Lily took over their mother's business creating essential oils from plants grown in their garden. Now they are learning the ropes while trying to keep the business afloat. Meanwhile, dairy farmer Jess is selling produce from her cows - who she considers to be her 'business partners'. Narrated by Denis Lawson

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: The dealers bid against each other on a pair of 1930s suitcases, a wind-up tin toy, a magic lantern, a silver condiment holder and a table and chair made from a fairground car

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones hosts the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Love is in the air as Ben, with a little help from Lexi and Lola, puts his engagement plan into action. However, Callum has doubts over his recent double-crossing of the Mitchells. Whitney tells Gray she has a date, so he plants a seed of doubt in her mind. Honey and Jay decide the time has come to tell Billy the truth about their relationship, while Chelsea convinces Lucas to make amends with Denise, Jack and Patrick

programme.title: Pooch Perfect
programme.desc: Sheridan Smith and her canine co-host Stanley open the doors of the Pooch Parlour to the last of the 16 dog groomers. First they tackle a quartet of cute Pomeranians, having to achieve a perfect pom-pom ball shape using scissors only. Then four party pooches hit the Dogwalk as a family of poodles and a Yorkshire terrier strut their stuff, having been given a makeover by the contestants. Who will judges Verity Hardcastle and Colin Taylor choose to go through to the quarter-finals?

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Neville tries to embrace island life with a game of beach volleyball, only for a rogue sandfly bite to land him in hospital. His time on the ward takes a shocking turn when a nurse takes her own life - although the DI suspects foul play and starts to build a case against his key suspects. Meanwhile, JP and Marlon are struggling to achieve a good working relationship

programme.title: The View
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers presents a review of the week's political news, including reports on the latest events at Stormont and Westminster. Plus, comment and analysis with studio guests

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: Katie Price: Harvey and Me
programme.desc: Model and businesswoman Katie Price's son Harvey was born with a rare disorder that affects brain function, hormones and vision, and he has a long list of medical and behavioural conditions that mean his future is unpredictable. This documentary follows Katie and Harvey as he turns 18, with the pair exploring the options of further education colleges and meeting other families that contain disabled young adults

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Gethin Jones hosts the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Dion Dublin take a look around a shell of a property in Bilston near Wolverhampton, a bungalow in Lower Quinton, near Stratford-upon-Avon, and an end terrace in in Gorton, Manchester

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Gill Johnson was convinced Australia would be the perfect place for her and her family to settle down. Husband Mark, however, was less than enthusiastic. Would a week in Perth persuade him that she wasn't just on a flight of fancy? Three years since their trial week, Nicki Chapman catches up with the pair to see whether they made the move Gill so desperately wanted

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood reports on how a detective's extraordinary ability to read body language brought a pair of international crooks to justice. Plus, the burglar who tried to batter his way into a chip shop and how inebriated night clubbers got a big shock when they took a short cut home

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Caroline Hawley and Phillip Serrell are on hand as two teams test their antiques knowledge at the Three Counties Showground in Malvern, Worcestershire. The competing duos then head to auction in Derbyshire. Presenter Charlie Ross oversees proceedings and also finds out about a classic car collection

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: After the body of Reginald Brody is discovered at the bottom of Helmsley Tower, Chief Inspector Valentine returns to Kembleford to investigate. Suspecting that there is more to his death than suicide, Valentine enlists Father Brown to help his inquiries. Mark Williams and Hugo Speer star in the crime drama

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Bakeation
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers reach Austria - the home of Mozart, the Sound of Music - on the fifth leg of their biking excursion across Europe. The trip starts in Vienna, where the pair unravel the mystery of the two competing Sachertorte recipes and taste the latest version - Sachertorte Reloaded

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley heads to Somerset to help a couple with a £310,000 budget find a country home where character charm and a practical utility room are top of the wish list. Along the way, Ginny heads into the Somerset Levels to see how bird wildlife efforts have brought the endangered crane back from near extinction

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Two Scottish farming families hope to make healthy sales at the Bowhouse market, Fife. To try to squeeze more income from their milk, husband and wife Robert and Jane, along with their son Ben now make 27 tonnes of award-winning cheese each year on their 660-acre dairy farm. Meanwhile, the Bruces gave up careers to start a small farm growing botanicals to make into spirits and liquors. Narrated by Denis Lawson

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers welcomes five more sellers to the Bidding Room, where they try to sell items to five professional dealers who are on the lookout for interesting pieces, with help from expert Simon Bower. Here, the dealers bid against each other on a vintage pub game, a small and mysterious statue and an apothecary set complete with tiny weights

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones presents the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich head to Hertfordshire to come up with design ideas for a large garden in Hemel Hempstead. Its owners, Carly and Andy, are keen to tackle an eyesore dating back to the Second World War

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic 4x100m gold medallist Mark Lewis-Francis, swimming great Rebecca Adlington and Scottish internationals in football and rugby union respectively Charlie Adam and John Barclay join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: A broken Billy snaps at Jay, and tells Lola about Jay and Honey - leaving his granddaughter in his bad books when he realises she already knew. Gray is happy to see how well Whitney is fitting in to her role and offers her a proposition, while Chelsea and Lucas wait for Jack

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedians Miles Jupp and Bob Mortimer and actresses Samantha Morton and Sarah Hadland join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds such as Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: When Lee joins Facebook for the first time and is contacted by an ex-girlfriend from 20 years ago, Lucy is annoyed that she cares so much. Her feelings are complicated by the sudden reappearance of a friend from University with whom she had a brief relationship. Threatened by fond memories from the past, Lee and Lucy start behaving badly. Comedy, starring Lee Mack and Sally Bretton

programme.title: Give My Head Peace
programme.desc: Uncle Andy is kidnapped and a huge ransom demand is made. However, his loved ones are left wondering whether he is worth a hundred grand. Award-winning comedy, starring Tim McGarry, Marty Reid and Paddy Jenkins

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by Hollywood star Sam Neill, who is appearing in new Australian comedy-drama Rams, and actress Billie Piper, who talks about her film Rare Beasts, which she wrote, directed and stars in. Cold Feet star James Nesbitt discusses his new Northern Ireland-set BBC series Bloodlands, while comedian Ando Osho promotes her debut novel Asking for a Friend. Plus, music legend Tom Jones chats and performs his new single No Hole in My Head

programme.title: Young Guns (1988)
programme.desc: Petty crook Billy the Kid is taken in by English farmer John Tunstall and given a job as a ranch hand. However, when Tunstall is murdered by a business rival, Billy and the rest of his 'regulators' set out to avenge his death, leading them into conflict with the law - and marking the start of Billy's notoriety as a gunslinger. Western, with Emilio Estevez, Charlie Sheen, Kiefer Sutherland and Lou Diamond Phillips

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Live Commons Digital, Culture, Media and Sport Sub-committee session on data ethics and online harms, with evidence from the information commissioner Elizabeth Denham

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Live Commons Health and Science & Technology committees joint session on lessons from the coronavirus pandemic, with evidence from Sir Simon Stevens, chief executive of NHS England

programme.desc: Coverage of yesterday's Downing Street Covid-19 news conference with the Prime Minister, Chief Medical Officer for England Professor Chris Whitty and NHS England Chief Executive Sir Simon Stevens.

programme.title: Do Black Lives Matter in Brazil?
programme.desc: In the year when Black Lives Matter became a rallying cry around the world, a BBC Brazil investigation discovered that in the first half of 2020, more people were killed by the police in the state of Rio de Janeiro state than in the whole of the USA. Of those killed, 75% were black Brazilians, many of whom were just teenagers. Ligia Guimaraes meets several families fighting for justice over the killing of their sons.

programme.title: Click
programme.desc: As remote medicine becomes more commonplace, Click looks at easy-to-use, medical-grade, diagnostic devices that are becoming available so family physicians can provide a full consultation remotely. They also visit a hospital in Madrid where AI is helping Covid patients in the ICU, who are in a coma, with pain management. The teams also look at a new breed of whiskies that are mixed and aged by AI.

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Rita is Crogall
programme.desc: Rita and Crocodile go on a skiing trip. They whizz up and down the mountains, but Crocodile is the fastest

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: When Bing and his friends draw a picture for Gilly, Charlie does a squiggle on Bing’s picture. But Gilly shows them that this is not such a bad thing

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: When Bing is at Sula’s house for a PJ Party, he gets frightened when he hears a large scratchy branch outside

programme.title: Tree Fu Tom
programme.desc: Tom arrives in time to help Twigs collect bark for his 'bark-book' - taking to the skies with the Kangaroo Spell to catch all that they need for his collection. However, their hard work is in vain when their collection is stolen

programme.title: Treud na Dlùth-choille - Grad-Naidheachd (Jungle Bunch)
programme.desc: Al and Bob learn a new game, but are not convinced it's as good as their old sport

programme.title: Stòiridh
programme.desc: Muireann Kelly reads Dà Uilebheist, written and illustrated by David McKee and translated into Gaelic by Jo Macdonald

programme.title: Kung Fu Panda

programme.title: Geamaichean Gorach
programme.desc: The gang are busy, but the day ends with disappointment

programme.title: Saidheans Sporsail (Backyard Science)
programme.desc: Fun science experiments, including how to make a set of pan pipes and smarten up a pavement with a picturesque paint job. Presented by Kerry Anne MacLeod and Allan MacDonald

programme.title: Dealbhan Fraoich (Heather's Portraits)
programme.desc: Heather Dewar draws a charcoal portrait of Fiona Cruickshank on Skye

programme.title: Speaking Our Language

programme.desc: A look at Germany in 1958, including the River Rhine and grape harvesting

programme.desc: Anna Macleod talks to guests about Le Mùirn by Catriona Murray, Poverty Safari by Darren McGarvey, and Prickly Pearls of Palestine: The People Behind the Politics by Hilda Reilly

programme.title: DIY le Donnie
programme.desc: In South Uist, a Lochboisdale football team have a range of football equipment but no suitable place to house it. Donnie MacLeod designs and builds a rugged store for footballs, cones and goals, ensuring it will withstand gales and wild weather

programme.title: Sgeul Slainte
programme.desc: A group of nurses gathers to discuss the job, share anecdotes and reflect on how the profession has changed since the Second World War

programme.title: Scottish Premiership Football
programme.desc: Dundee United v St Mirren. Full-match coverage of the top-flight clash at Tannadice. With commentary from Alex O’Henley

programme.title: Tha Thu air Aigeann M'Inntinn
programme.desc: A hand-drawn animation by Catrìona Black about the Iolaire disaster and its lasting impact on the Lewis community, with first-hand accounts from survivors and witnesses and a haunting musical score by Sarah-Jane Summers

programme.title: Celtic Connections
programme.desc: Ross Couper and Tom Oakes perform one of their sets rooted in Scottish and Irish traditions

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Ceitidh Morag (Katie Morag)
programme.desc: Part one of two. Granaidh Baile disappears just before the wedding, but then returns with a very special visitor

programme.title: 'S E Iasg a Th'Annam (I'm a Fish)
programme.desc: The Bat Fish tells us how they aren’t good swimmers but that they use a horn on top of their heads with bait attached to it to attract prey

programme.title: Shane an Chef
programme.desc: Coconuts and calypso are on the menu in Munchington

programme.title: Abadas
programme.desc: The Abadas find out which one of them will be chosen to go on a fun and exciting journey to find today's word

programme.title: Rita is Crogall
programme.desc: Rita and Crocodile go on a skiing trip. They whizz up and down the mountains, but Crocodile is the fastest

programme.title: Stòiridh
programme.desc: Michael Hill reads Na Trioblaidean aig Sioraf, by Jory John, illustrated by Lane Smith and translated into Gaelic by Morag Stewart

programme.title: Donnie Murdo (Danger Mouse)
programme.desc: Baron Greenback brews a plot to steal every beverage on the planet and mix them together, turning Earth's drinks into a disgusting sludge

programme.title: Ronia, Nighean a' Mheirlich/Ronja, the Robber's Daughter
programme.desc: Ronia hears new information about her father when Mata and Borka’s robbers argue at Dìg an Dealanaich

programme.title: Geamaichean Gorach
programme.desc: Things go awry with the Competitors but Crùbag is near to hand, with lots of rhythm and the “cha cha cha”

programme.title: Port Pàipeir
programme.desc: Matilda has the power to always win in gambling, and starts betting obsessively, and accepts the invitation to play and gamble from a stranger who arrives at Paper Port

programme.title: Turas a' Bhradain (The Salmon's Journey)
programme.desc: Neen MacKay explores the scenery of the west coast, along with the region's salmon-fishing rivers

programme.title: Speaking Our Language
programme.desc: An opportunity to learn Gaelic. Presented by Rhoda MacDonald

programme.title: Earrann Eachdraidh (History Shorts)
programme.desc: An investigations into revolutions and uprisings throughout history, and the new beginnings they have the potential to inspire

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news and information from BBC ALBA

programme.title: Eòrpa (European Current Affairs)
programme.desc: A report on plans to build a space port on the Moine Peninsula in Sutherland, and whether Germany has dealt with Covid-19 in a better way than many other countries

programme.title: Na Gleidheadairean (The Animal Keepers)
programme.desc: At Highland Wildlife Park, Vickie attempts a training session with Hamish the polar bear, but he is more interested in playing

programme.title: Ceol Duthchasach/Ken Burns' Country Music
programme.desc: The history of the art form and how it evolved between 1953 and 1963, when the confluence of blues and hillbilly music gave birth to rockabilly, the precursor of rock 'n' roll

programme.title: Dhan Uisge
programme.desc: Calum Maclean explains his passion for wild swimming in remote and unspoilt lochs and rivers, beginning with a visit to the River Douchary in the north-west Highlands

programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Leugh le Linda
programme.desc: Linda Macleod travels to Stornoway where she reads Luchag Bheag aig Sgoil Sholais na Gealaich with nursery school pupils.

programme.title: Creag nam Buthaidean (Puffin Rock)
programme.desc: Oona is very excited as she gets the chance to show Baba a rainbow for the first time

programme.title: Padraig Post: Seirbheis Deis agus Sonraichte
programme.desc: Pàdraig's deliveries are delayed when a package of carwash rollers falls from the back of the van

programme.title: Nannag a' Noo/Huggleboo
programme.desc: Huggleboo?discovers a beautiful butterfly, she calls him Wing but the butterfly doesn’t move anymore.?Could?he?be?broken?

programme.title: Stòiridh
programme.desc: Pàdruig Morrison reads An Latha Spòrs, written and illustrated by Nick Butterworth and Mick Inkpen, and translated into Gaelic by Jo Macdonald

programme.title: Aithne air Ainmhidhean (All About Animals)
programme.desc: Interesting facts about Aleesha, a baby American alligator that is queen of its swamp and one of a clutch of 40 brothers and sisters

programme.title: Geamaichean Gorach
programme.desc: Coinneach Clogaid is trying to win as usual, but things don’t go his way

programme.title: Na Moomins/Moomin Valley
programme.desc: Mùmantrobha turns his hand to be a detective after Mamaidh Mùman is placed under arrest and under suspicion of murdering Mrs Fhaoineastan

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news and information from BBC ALBA

programme.title: Fraochy Bay
programme.desc: The community's annual talent goes into underdrive as various acts perform to sporadic and varied applause. All goes reasonably badly until Ina's dog Toodles contracts a severe flea infestation from the Flea Circus stars. Will someone save the day?

programme.title: Machair
programme.desc: Gaelic soap based on the Isle of Lewis

programme.title: Cearcall
programme.desc: Donald Morrison takes a look through the archive of the popular current affairs series, recalling the start of the Ret debate and how Scalpay was affected by the decline in fishing

programme.title: Gàrradh Phàdruig
programme.desc: Peter MacQueen prepares the bed for planting out the brassicas, pots some tomatoes and upcycles a discarded container to use as a planter for baby lettuce

programme.title: BBC Radio Scotland Young Traditional Musician 2021
programme.desc: Some of Scotland's finest young musicians battle it out in the semi-finals of BBC Radio Scotland's Young Traditional Musician of The Year Award. Recorded last October

programme.title: An t-Inneal Espresso/The Espresso Machine
programme.desc: Drama revolving around a high-end espresso machine. Ealasaid loaned her machine to Ailig's girlfriend Una, and desperately wants it back. Unfortunately, Ailig and Una just broke up

programme.title: State of Happiness
programme.desc: Phillips Petroleum introduces a smoking ban on the Ocean Viking rig, while the mayor of Stavanger speculates on whether the Americans might have found oil. Toril Torstensen makes an unavoidable choice. In Norwegian and English

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chasers Road Trip: Trains, Brains and Automobiles
programme.desc: Anne Hegerty, Mark Labbett and Shaun Wallace - better known to The Chase fans as the Governess, the Beast and the Dark Destroyer - continue their investigation into different types of intelligence by going head to head with child geniuses in the UK. Along the way, they delve deeper into IQ, find out about the link between emotional intelligence and creativity, and learn what sleep can tell a person about their intelligence

programme.title: ITV News at Ten

programme.title: 63 Up
programme.desc: The late director Michael Apted catches up with a group of Britons whose lives have been chronicled on film since the age of seven. Here he is reunited with East Ender Tony, who originally dreamed of being a jockey. Last time he was in Spain, with plans of being a property developer, but where is he now? Apted also catches up with Nick, the shy Yorkshire farm boy who eventually moved Stateside. Nick reflects on family, health and life in Trump's America

programme.title: Finding Alice
programme.desc: Meeting George leaves Alice shaken. He does look like Harry, but why didn't he show up when Harry was alive? Before Alice can deal with that revelation, first she must arrange a funeral for the man she loved. Meanwhile, the police continue to look for an unidentified witness captured on the CCTV of Harry's fall. Drama, starring Keeley Hawes, Gemma Jones and Kenneth Cranham

Along the way, they delve deeper into IQ, find out about the link between emotional intelligence and creativity, and learn what sleep can tell a person about their intelligence

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: 63 Up
programme.desc: The late director Michael Apted catches up with a group of Britons whose lives have been chronicled on film since the age of seven. Here he is reunited with East Ender Tony, who originally dreamed of being a jockey. Last time he was in Spain, with plans of being a property developer, but where is he now? Apted also catches up with Nick, the shy Yorkshire farm boy who eventually moved Stateside. Nick reflects on family, health and life in Trump's America

programme.title: Finding Alice
programme.desc: Meeting George leaves Alice shaken. He does look like Harry, but why didn't he show up when Harry was alive? Before Alice can deal with that revelation, first she must arrange a funeral for the man she loved. Meanwhile, the police continue to look for an unidentified witness captured on the CCTV of Harry's fall. Drama, starring Keeley Hawes, Gemma Jones and Kenneth Cranham

programme.title: FYI Extra
programme.desc: A short bulletin rounding up the day's stories

programme.title: Tonight
programme.desc: With record global temperatures and extreme weather events affecting all parts of the planet, there seems to be little argument that the climate is changing. The UK government is promising urgent action and wants everyone to go greener. But what can the public really do to help? Meteorologist and weather presenter Laura Tobin investigates

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.title: Lingo
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Adil Ray in which husband and wife Tony and Caroline from Bromley, south London, San Francisco-born Eric and his husband Russell, and childhood sweethearts Ruka and Ibrahim from Peckham, south London, use their powers of deduction to work out words that appear in the Lingo grids and Puzzlewords. The last remaining duo have the opportunity to win the money they've banked earlier in the show to potentially double their winnings, or leaving with nothing

programme.desc: A crackdown on an organised crime racket leads Foyle and Milner to a shipyard owned by the Talbot brothers, a duo with dubious links to a communist agitator who has recently moved to the area. When an engineer is murdered just hours after working at the shipyard, the detectives desperately try to track down the killer and discover the truth surrounding the Talbots' shady business dealings. Peter Capaldi guest stars in the wartime drama, with Michael Kitchen, Anthony Howell and Honeysuckle Weeks

programme.desc: The bodies of a glamorous senior citizen and a distinguished doctor are found beside a river - and although suspicion for their murders initially falls on poachers, Barnaby suspects a more monetary motive for the attack. With Honor Blackman, Philip Franks, James Hazeldine and Jeff Rawle

programme.desc: An FBI agent crosses swords with Poirot while trying to retrieve the American Navy's top-secret plans for a new submarine, which have been stolen by a glamorous nightclub singer with a sideline in espionage. Drama, starring David Suchet

programme.desc: A commercial decision to turn Midsomer Deverell's memorial garden into a tea shop proves fatal for members of the Inkpen family. Wartime crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen, Anthony Howell, Honeysuckle Weeks and Keith Barron

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Julia McKenzie returns as the sleuthing spinster in the first of three murder mysteries. While staying at a hotel in the Caribbean, Miss Marple investigates the death of a fellow guest, refusing to believe he died of a heart attack. With the help of a business tycoon she sets about finding the real cause of the man's demise, and is soon drawn into a web of deceit and dark magic. Adapted by Charlie Higson and co-starring Antony Sher, Robert Webb, Montserrat Lombard, Daniel Rigby, Hermione Norris, MyAnna Buring, Warren Brown and Oliver Ford Davies

programme.desc: The detective investigates the death of a pensioner found stabbed on a train at the height of rush hour. The trail leads the team to a quiet Northumberland coastal town where clues appear to lead to one particular street - but why are the residents so reluctant to talk? Starring Brenda Blethyn, with David Leon, Sonya Cassidy, Jon Morrison and Eva Birthistle

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jon informs Deirdre that he is going to tell his ex-wife about them. Sally tells Natalie that Kevin wanted to go back to her but she turned him down. Chris is jealous of Des

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Vera tells Jack she is not doing any work until he gets rid of Natalie. Gary gets cold feet over saying he is Katie's father. Chris accuses Angie of not wanting to live with him

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A stately home is turned into a theme park celebrating the work of local horror writer Ellis Bell, but the opening ceremony is marred by the death of a film star. Barnaby and Troy's investigation uncovers the secrets within an eccentric family, and a centuries-old village feud comes under the spotlight. Murder mystery, written by Alan Plater and guest starring Beth Goddard

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A Belgian film star arrives in London and summons her friend Poirot to unravel the mysterious threats she has been receiving, which demand the return of a priceless diamond, the Western Star. Rosalind Bennett guest stars

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: Charles investigates the fatal stabbing of a businessman in a pub car park - and gradually discovers the victim was not the upstanding pillar of the community he appeared to be. Jack Shepherd and Sharon Duce star

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Pauline is all set to achieve her ambition when she is given the chance to join the Portwenn lifeboat crew. However, her joy is short-lived when she discovers the chauvinistic tendencies of her shipmates. Martin is accused of murder by the husband of a woman who died in his care. The irate man resorts to sabotaging Aunt Joan's crops in retaliation but an unexpected twist reveals the true motivation for his ire

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: The doctor is surprised when his parents visit out of the blue, but they bring unwelcome tidings for all, especially Aunt Joan, who may be driven from her home. School secretary Maureen receives better news when what she thought was her thyroid problem turns out to be a pregnancy. Louisa calls Martin after Danny collapses - but the ambulance is delayed and emergency surgery must be performed to save his life

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: When Grace Phillips is killed in an explosion in a munitions factory, Foyle is asked by one of her co-workers to investigate the death. Initially he concludes a tragic accident has occurred until another woman is found murdered and Foyle starts to believe the deaths are connected. Wartime drama, starring Michael Kitchen

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Newlyweds Mike and Ellie decide to build their dream home at a beauty spot called Gypsy's Acre, ignoring the warnings of elusive romany Mrs Lee about an ancient curse supposedly cast upon the land. The couple settle into their new life, but the mysterious woman makes her presence felt, subjecting them to a campaign of hatred in an attempt to force them out. Tragedy soon strikes, but as luck would have it Miss Marple is staying nearby with her recently widowed old friend Mrs Phillpot. However, if the spinster sleuth is to solve the puzzle, she must put her own life in grave danger. Murder mystery, starring Julia McKenzie, with Wendy Craig, Glynis Barber, Hugh Dennis, Tamzin Outhwaite, Tom Hughes, Joanna Vanderham, Aneurin Barnard, William Hope and Janet Henfrey

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Natalie gets an alarming letter from Nick's solicitor. Kevin asks Sally to accept less money but she refuses. Des begs Samantha to stay in Weatherfield

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Ned worries about the blood tests. Eric takes drastic measures when he finds Kathy celebrating taking over ownership of the wine bar. Zak and Butch attempt to find some rent money

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Marlon confronts Eric about dousing the wine bar in paraffin. Kirsty admits to making herself sick, and Scott tells Kelly he is not going back to the army

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A notorious burglar escapes from prison and returns to Aidensfield to pursue a vendetta against Ashfordly police. Roping in two local boys, the villain dupes them into helping him carry out his plan while PC Mason desperately tries to find him before he strikes again. Dawn sets Joyce up on a date with PC Younger - but is oblivious to the fact that the young copper actually has feelings for her. George Cole guest stars, with Joe McFadden and John Duttine

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid is in for a shock when he finds out who Jean has enlisted to decorate Mike's bedroom

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: Henpecked George finally finds a job, but the line of work could make him unpopular with road users. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: A crackdown on an organised crime racket leads Foyle and Milner to a shipyard owned by the Talbot brothers, a duo with dubious links to a communist agitator who has recently moved to the area. When an engineer is murdered just hours after working at the shipyard, the detectives desperately try to track down the killer and discover the truth surrounding the Talbots' shady business dealings. Peter Capaldi guest stars in the wartime drama, with Michael Kitchen, Anthony Howell and Honeysuckle Weeks

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Carol tries to help struggling single mum Barbara while dealing with the news that a woman claiming to be her own mother has arrived in Aidensfield. The nurse is then taken aback when Janet has the idea of reuniting them. Meanwhile, Mason's fears for Barbara are proved right after a shocking turn of events, and Wetherby and Younger enlist Peggy to search for the chief constable's missing dog

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Marlon tries to cover up the smell of paraffin before Kathy arrives for work. Heather is determined to find a new home. Marlon tells Lyn the truth about the night before

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Biff is shocked to discover Tara is evicting the Glovers. Kirsty insists she is pleased her bulimia is out in the open now. Zoe offers to help Ned and the Dingles

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Kevin tells Chris that he has left Natalie, and he asks Sally to take him back. Zoe goes into premature labour after drinking with Leanne

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Kevin attempts to prove to Sally that he's never going back to Natalie, and Gail advises Sally to forgive him. Gary is tearful when Zoe gives birth to a girl

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The bodies of a glamorous senior citizen and a distinguished doctor are found beside a river - and although suspicion for their murders initially falls on poachers, Barnaby suspects a more monetary motive for the attack. However, the case becomes complicated with the discovery that a local restaurateur, who had recently learnt his wife had been having an affair with the male victim, has also met an untimely end. With Honor Blackman, Philip Franks, James Hazeldine and Jeff Rawle

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An FBI agent crosses swords with Poirot while trying to retrieve the American Navy's top-secret plans for a new submarine, which have been stolen by a glamorous nightclub singer with a sideline in espionage. Drama, starring David Suchet

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: When a woman is found strangled, a pathologist detects chilling similarities with a number of unsolved murders - prompting Wycliffe and his team to reopen 11-year-old case files. Detective drama, starring Jack Shepherd, Tim Wylton and Helen Masters

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A commercial decision to turn Midsomer Deverell's memorial garden into a tea shop proves fatal for members of the Inkpen family. DCI Barnaby's investigation unearths more than mere local hostility as he lifts the lid on long-buried secrets. Anna Calder-Marshall and Margaret Tyzack guest star

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: American GIs arrive in Hastings to a mixed welcome and Foyle is called on to calm the locals. But his attempts at soothing relations are jeopardised when a barmaid's involvement with a visiting soldier results in murder, leading the detective to defy orders to keep the peace and solve the case. Wartime crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen, Anthony Howell, Honeysuckle Weeks and Keith Barron

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Julia McKenzie returns as the sleuthing spinster in the first of three murder mysteries. While staying at a hotel in the Caribbean, Miss Marple investigates the death of a fellow guest, refusing to believe he died of a heart attack. With the help of a business tycoon she sets about finding the real cause of the man's demise, and is soon drawn into a web of deceit and dark magic. Adapted by Charlie Higson and co-starring Antony Sher, Robert Webb, Montserrat Lombard, Daniel Rigby, Hermione Norris, MyAnna Buring, Warren Brown and Oliver Ford Davies

programme.desc: Betty refuses to believe that Zoe had nothing to do with the evictions. Kathy begins her alterations to the tearooms. Kirsty is scared about seeing a counsellor

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Vera signs up for an evening catering course. Judy wants Gary to claim to be the baby's father so she can legally live with them. Sally tells Kevin she wants him back

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jon is startled to hear that Deirdre is planning to move into his house. Vera is horrified to find Alec has taken Natalie on. The Malletts collect Zoe and Katie from hospital

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Troy investigate the death of amateur artist Ruth Fairfax, whose body has been discovered by Joyce - but the more they delve into the dead woman's background, the more mysterious she becomes. Detective drama, starring John Nettles, with guest appearances by John Sessions and Leslie Phillips

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is called in to investigate when the UK's prime minister and his secretary are kidnapped in France. However, he has only a day and a half to solve the case before an important conference is set to take place. Drama, starring David Suchet

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The detective and his team investigate a series of bizarre events in the aftermath of a wedding which boasted a most unusual guest list. It transpires the couple had only met three weeks before the ceremony, and the honeymoon in a luxury hotel ends in a brutal killing. Drama, starring Jack Shepherd, with Ronald Hines and Josephine Butler

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective investigates the death of a pensioner found stabbed on a train at the height of rush hour. The case has a huge impact on Joe's home life when his daughter is named as a key witness, and he struggles to maintain a professional distance. The trail leads the team to a quiet Northumberland coastal town where clues appear to lead to one particular street - but why are the residents so reluctant to talk? Starring Brenda Blethyn, with David Leon, Sonya Cassidy, Jon Morrison and Eva Birthistle

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth helps an old friend find refuge at country house Greenshaw's Folly, but a sinister shadow falls over the strange building's various inhabitants and a murderer strikes. Miss Marple uncovers secrets from the past about Miss Greenshaw as a dangerous storm gathers and the bodies start to pile up. Julia McKenzie heads an all-star cast that includes Jim Moir (better known as Vic Reeves), Kimberley Nixon, Fiona Shaw, Robert Glenister, Julia Sawalha, Judy Parfitt and Matt Willis (formerly of pop group Busted)

programme.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: A huge scandal threatens Bohemia, as Holmes is outwitted by a bewitching woman's blackmail who owns a compromising photograph of a European royal. Period detective drama, starring Jeremy Brett as the brilliant Baker Street sleuth, with David Burke as his trusty companion Dr Watson, guest starring Gayle Hunnicutt

programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace
programme.desc: Harriet poses as a journalist in an attempt to track a notorious criminal trying to muscle in on London's organised crime scene, and soon becomes embroiled in the machinations of a crafty French jewel thief. Police drama, starring Michael Brandon and Glynis Barber

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Carter's wife Alison is targeted by a hit-and-run driver while performing a routine errand on his behalf. Police investigations reveal she was wearing the coat of a fellow teacher with dubious connections. Patrick Troughton and Liz Smith guest star, with John Thaw and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: The brother of the late Clement Armstrong devises a plan to avenge his death at the hands of the cybernauts by setting a deadly trap for Steed and Emma. Peter Cushing guest stars

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur becomes involved with a gambler, whose sudden death leaves the dodgy car dealer £2,000 worse off. Meanwhile, Terry helps his old flame Sylvie leave her abusive husband. Billy Connolly guest stars in the vintage comedy drama, alongside George Cole and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Motorbike Show
programme.desc: Henry Cole tours Majorca on a classic bike, begins the restoration of a vintage Harley-Davidson, and visits Bike Shed in London, the home of the New Wave custom scene

programme.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: Strange, seemingly malevolent encoded messages sour a wealthy man's wedded bliss, prompting him to seek Holmes's expertise. Victorian mystery, starring Jeremy Brett and David Burke

programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace
programme.desc: Dempsey finds himself the target of a crazed female admirer. Guest starring Elizabeth Sladen and Suzi Quatro

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan and Carter are confronted by a reporter with information on the unsolved Golden Maid robbery. However, the newsman's strange behaviour raises more questions than answers. Classic police drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: Steed and Mrs Peel infiltrate an international conference at which the delegates have begun acting strangely - by predicting their own deaths. Patrick Macnee and Diana Rigg star, with Clifford Evans, William Lucas and Allan Cuthbertson

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: A tape implicating two senior police officers in a criminal conspiracy is mistakenly delivered to Daley Videos, and Arthur and Terry find themselves placed in contact with its owner - a local major crime boss. Ian McShane, Rula Lenska, Brian Blessed and Ray Winstone guest star

programme.title: The Motorbike Show
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Sam Lovegrove continue their rebuild of a Harley WLC. Henry also visits the Norton factory at Donington and finds out about the iconic Sportser bike

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Emmerdale actor Dominic Brunt (Paddy Kirk), dancer and choreographer Wayne Sleep, Royle Family star Ricky Tomlinson and Good Morning Britain's Charlotte Hawkins compete to win thousands of pounds for charity by taking on a trivia expert in a test of their general knowledge. However, they must work as a team to build their prize pot and stay one step ahead of the Chaser. Bradley Walsh asks the questions

programme.title: Monster Carp
programme.desc: The team head to the Netherlands, which is renowned for its extensive waterways, but they will be focusing their attention on a lake called Carplantis which is home to some fantastic-looking, and very large carp. Plus, they also take in a spot of barbel fishing on the River Meuse

programme.title: Giant Lobster Hunters
programme.desc: With Tabor injured and stuck at port, Squizzy enlists Bryce to learn the ropes and how to win in the lobster game. Plus, Skipper Jay has an order for 250 kilograms to fulfill

programme.title: Mad Max 2 (1981)
programme.desc: A former cop haunted by his tragic past roams the wasteland of Australia in the aftermath of a nuclear war. Futuristic adventure sequel, starring Mel Gibson, Bruce Spence, Vernon Wells, Emil Minty and Kjell Nilsson

However, this pioneering construction seems an exercise in trial and error from the start, and what began as a seemingly simple, pure concept becomes a complicated feat of engineering

In London, Roscoe walks out of parents' home, while Colin arrives from Wales to start a new career on Savile Row. The three men meet, and along with fellow student Ash and best friend Jill form a gang and move into a flat, christened the Pink Palace. However, a distant threat on the horizon means that life in the 1980s might be all too precious. Olly Alexander, Omari Douglas and Callum Scott Howells star

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Belfast, flame-haired hairbrush executive Julie hopes to charm her way to the £1,000 prize with an evening of fusion food, drawing on her experiences from living in Japan and Hong Kong and serving her own versions of takeaway classics

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Belfast, mobile barista Justin hopes to wow his guests with a barbecue extravaganza; he's even doing his dessert on the barbie. programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Belfast, executive assistant Cushla brings out her serious side as she plans to win over her guests with impressive cuisine. programme.title: Come Dine with Me

Sonia is keen to set up her own Indian food business, so she needs a large kitchen, and Andrew wants the home to be ultra-modern in design. Laura Hamilton sets out to find five properties within their £145,000 budget that satisfy both their demands

Harry Hill hosts, alongside judges Liam Charles and Ravneet Gill

programme.title: The Nightmare Neighbour Next Door
programme.desc: The documentary focuses on two residential disputes that escalated from complaints about noise-making to far more serious quarrels. Warren and Sharee detail how their peaceful lives in a quiet Birmingham suburb were ruined when new neighbour Harry moved in. Harry complained of noise the pair knew wasn't coming from their household, an accusation he would level more than once, and with increasing intensity. Finally, when the hostile Harry pulled out a gun and threatened the couple over their garden fence, they were forced to sell up and move out. However, even after relocating, it seemed their nightmare was not over. Also featured is the story of Jill, who was just a teenager when Barry, who lived with his parents next-door, became fixated on her family making a racket. Brushing off the grievance, Jill and her relatives tried to get on with their lives, but one autumn day the feud took a tragic, life-changing turn

programme.title: Natural History Museum: World of Wonder
programme.desc: Leading experts examine a rock in a crate of finds from an archaeological dig in Africa, which could contain a very rare 200-million-year-old dinosaur skull. The museum staff prepares for the Wildlife Photographer of the Year awards. Other artefacts from the museum featured in this episode include meteorite fragments that are older than the solar system and the oldest complete beetle specimens ever found in the UK

programme.title: Robson Green: Walking Coast to Coast
programme.desc: The actor makes an early start for the remaining 46 miles of his trek along Hadrian's Wall, crossing the border from Northumbria to Cumbria at Gilslan, where he finds playful graffiti from the Romans. Moving on, he learns how to link a Cumbrian sausage, takes to the skies for a bird's-eye view of Carlisle and finally reaches the end of his journey at Bowness-on-Solway

programme.title: Tortured By Mum and Dad? The Turpin 13
programme.desc: This documentary investigates the shocking story of David and Louise Turpin, who kept their 13 children imprisoned in their own home until they were found in January 2018 when one of the siblings escaped and called the police. This programme shines a light on the mystery of how an unassuming suburban family in California managed to hide their dark and sinister secret for so long

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain
programme.desc: A look at the events surrounding Raoul Moat's shooting spree in Northumberland during the summer of 2010 and the huge manhunt that led to his arrest. The former convict, bouncer and tree surgeon shot his former partner and killed her boyfriend and then shot and blinded a policeman while on the run
